rationale: In the citing PDF, Ding (2007) is cited where the authors introduce Study 3 as an incentive-aligned conjoint experiment, rather than as a generic literature-list reference. The citation is used to justify and frame adding real consequences to conjoint choices as a methodological condition. While the paper's core contribution is about discriminability and scale expansion, Ding (2007) meaningfully informs this robustness-test design choice.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the incentive-aligned conjoint idea associated with Ding (2007), together with related prior work, to run a real-incentive version of its conjoint experiment. This is used to test whether the paper's scale-discriminability effects hold under incentive-compatible conditions.
